title: Performance Review Process and Self-Assessment Tips
text:
Q: What is the performance review process, timeline, and how can I effectively complete my self-assessment?
A: The performance review process occurs annually, typically starting in January and concluding by the end of March. Employees will receive a notification in early January to begin their self-assessment, which should be submitted by the end of January. Managers will then review the self-assessments and provide feedback during one-on-one meetings scheduled throughout February. Final performance reviews will be completed by the end of March. To effectively complete your self-assessment, reflect on your accomplishments, challenges, and areas for growth over the past year. Use specific examples and data to support your claims, and align your goals with the company's objectives. For additional guidance, refer to the self-assessment template available on the HR portal or contact your HR representative for assistance.
